free_text: Low-yield products from fission of Th232, U235, and
U238 with 14.8-MeV neutrons
ICT neutron generator.
The neutrons are produced by the reaction of
deuterons on tritium in a rotating target assembly.
Gas-flow beta proportional counters.
NaI(Tl) gamma detectors.
The irradiations were usually 8 h in
length, producing up to 4x10**12 fissions in the
Th232, 3x10**13 fissions in the U235, and 1.5x10**13
fissions in the U238. After the neutron irradiations,
the target foils were dissolved in the presence of
10-20-mg amounts of carriers for each of the desired
products. The samples were counted on gas-flow beta
proportional counters or NaI(Tl) gamma counters.
Flux monitor.
Data uncertainties given include
uncertainty in counting efficiencies and counting
statistics, and standard deviation. Not included is
6% uncertainty in number of fissions.
Table II, page 1254.
VM
